targets.rawSql:
SELECT
  $__timeGroupAlias(created_at, $__interval),
  COUNT(id) AS "Accepted"
FROM notifications
WHERE
  $__timeFilter(created_at)
GROUP BY 1
ORDER BY 1;

SELECT
  $__timeGroupAlias(created_at, $__interval),
  COUNT(id) AS "Delivered"
FROM notifications
WHERE
  $__timeFilter(created_at) AND status = 'DELIVERED'
GROUP BY 1
ORDER BY 1;

SELECT
  $__timeGroupAlias(attempted_at, $__interval, 0),
  SUM(CASE WHEN status = 'SUCCESS' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS "Successful Deliveries",
  SUM(CASE WHEN status = 'FAILED' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS "Failed Deliveries"
FROM (
  -- 1. Grab your actual data
  SELECT attempted_at, status
  FROM delivery_attempts
  WHERE $__timeFilter(attempted_at)
  
  UNION ALL
  
  -- 2. Inject a dummy row at the start of your graph so Grafana's fill(0) always triggers
  SELECT $__timeFrom()::timestamp AS attempted_at, 'DUMMY' AS status
) AS combined_data
GROUP BY 1
ORDER BY 1;

panels.title: Delivery Success vs Failure Rate Over Time:

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
  $__timeGroupAlias(attempted_at, $__interval),
  AVG(duration_ms) AS "Average Delivery Duration (ms)"
FROM delivery_attempts
WHERE
  $__timeFilter(attempted_at)
GROUP BY 1
ORDER BY 1;

panels.description: Messages Delivered in set timeframe
panels.title: Accepted in TimeFrame

panels.description: Messages Delivered in set timeframe

targets.rawSql:
SELECT COUNT(id) 
FROM notifications 
WHERE $__timeFilter(created_at) and status = 'DELIVERED'

panels.title: Delivered in TimeFrame

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
  $__timeGroupAlias(updated_at, $__interval, 0),
  COUNT(*) AS updates
FROM notifications
WHERE $__timeFilter(updated_at)
GROUP BY 1
ORDER BY 1
